title: Resource Inventory, Delivery Evidence, Budgets, and Performance Boundaries · Inference Brief: Performance Nonclaim
summary: The audit sums HTML attributes as transfer size, confuses lab and field data, ignores cache and render context, and claims user performance from a crawler timer. Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler ts performance nonclaim increment in bounded resource and performance evidence report. Learners progress through predic…

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Predict Performance Nonclaim · Theory Performance Nonclaim
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 5746 during an authorized municipal service audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile resource graphs, size dimensions, cache context, budgets, and static-versus-user performance claims; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then reorder one frontier, p-limit, Promise, AbortSignal, stream, retry, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Performance Nonclaim model · Theory Performance…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Crawler timing can replace browser metrics and field data.
